note: source.url https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32017R0745 is the canonical EUR-Lex page for MDR 2017/745 (OJ L 117, 5.5.2017) — confirmed valid. MDR Annex VIII with 22 classification rules (Class I/IIa/IIb/III) is confirmed by EC official guidance (MDCG 2021-24) and EUR-Lex consolidated text. Article 51 = device classification mandate (4 classes per intended purpose/risk); classification-fragment cites 'Articles 51–54 — Classification Procedure' which is broadly correct: Art 51 classification, Art 52 conformity routes, Art 53 reclassification disputes, Art 54 voluntary consultation — all in the same chapter. NMPA classification catalogue reference marked [UNVERIFIED — verify current version] which is appropriate; 2017 Announcement No. 104 was valid at publication but has had addenda. mandatory_status is accurate for both EU and CN sides. Gap description accurately captures the independence of the two classification systems and market-access risk of misclassification.

source.url confirmed canonical (same MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). Conformity assessment routes by class (Art 52, Annex IX/X/XI) confirmed by official EC manufacturer factsheet (health.ec.europa.eu) and EUR-Lex search. Class I self-declare (except sterile/measuring/reusable surgical); IIa: Annex IX or XI-A+XI-B; IIb: Annex IX or X+XI; III: Annex IX or X+XI with design dossier — all correct per MDR. NB must be EU-designated via EUDAMED — confirmed. CN GMP reference '2014/2022 revision' in cn_common_equivalent standards_or_laws is a minor error: NMPA GMP (Order No. 64) is the 2014 version; there was no formal Order 64 revision in 2022 (a 2025 revision, NMPA Order No. 107, was issued Nov 2025, effective Nov 1 2026). Gap NB timeline '[UNVERIFIED on current timelines]' is self-flagged appropriately. mandatory_status accurate.

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). MDR Art 52 conformity assessment routes confirmed by EC manufacturer factsheet and EUR-Lex. Class I self-declaration (with named exceptions), IIa/IIb/III NB mandatory — correct. Annex II (Technical Documentation) and Annex III (Post-Market Surveillance Technical Documentation) citations are correct per MDR structure. Annex X (type-examination) and Annex XI (product conformity verification) references confirmed. NB timeline 12–36 months in gap text is industry-reported range — self-flagged uncertainty is appropriate. CMDE description as government-designated review centre is accurate. mandatory_status correct.

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). Clinical evaluation under Annex XIV confirmed by EC MDCG 2020-5 and EUR-Lex. Article 61 (clinical evaluation obligation for all classes) and Articles 62–82 (clinical investigations) confirmed by official MDCG guidance search. PMCF obligation and stricter equivalence criteria (same manufacturer + documented access to technical file) are accurately described per Annex XIV Part A Section 3. MDCG 2020-5 and MDCG 2020-6 references are real official documents available on health.ec.europa.eu. CN NMPA clinical evaluation guidance (2021) and State Council Order 739 Art 24 references plausible. mandatory_status correct ('Mandatory for all device classes' for EU, 'Class II and III' for CN).

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). MDR Art 10(9) QMS requirement confirmed by EC QMS helpdesk page and official manufacturer factsheet. Annex IX (QMS conformity) and Annex XI (product conformity verification) references correct. ISO 13485:2016 alignment confirmed. STANDARD ERROR in cn_common_equivalent.standards_or_laws: '醫療器械生產質量管理規範 (NMPA 2014, revised 2022)' — the 2022 revision claim is incorrect. NMPA GMP is Order No. 64, issued 2014. There was no promulgated 2022 revision; the next full revision (NMPA Order No. 107) was issued November 2025, effective November 1 2026. The '2022 revision' notation is a factual error and should be removed or replaced with '(NMPA 2014, Order No. 64; revised edition NMPA Order No. 107, Nov 2025, effective Nov 2026)'. mandatory_status accurate. Gap description (annual surveillance + 5-year recertification, English-language QMS docs, NB fees) is accurate.

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). CE marking, NB involvement (Art 52), EUDAMED actor/UDI registration (Arts 27–30), EU Authorised Representative (Art 11), PMS/PSUR obligations (Arts 83–100), importer/distributor obligations (Arts 13–14) all confirmed by official EC manufacturer factsheet and EUR-Lex. STANDARD CITATION ERROR: 'Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/2078 (EUDAMED actor registration)' in standards_or_laws — CIR 2021/2078 covers EUDAMED operational rules broadly, but actor registration is also governed by Annex VI MDR and CIR 2017/2185 (NB codes). CIR 2021/2078 is a plausible citation for EUDAMED rules. EUDAMED URL note: the fragment does not cite the EUDAMED URL but the udi-002 row does (see that row for URL error). NB timeline '[UNVERIFIED exact figures]' is correctly self-flagged. EUDAMED mandatory status should reflect that four modules became mandatory from 28 May 2026 per Commission Decision (EU) 2025/2371 — not yet reflected in this row but row was not purporting to state that specific date. mandatory_status accurate.

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). MDR Art 10(9) QMS, EN ISO 13485:2016, Annex IX confirmed. Articles 87–92 vigilance reference is plausible (MDR Arts 87–92 cover vigilance reporting). STANDARD ERROR in cn_common_equivalent.standards_or_laws: '医疗器械生产质量管理规范 (NMPA GMP) — Order No. 64 (2022 revision)' — the '2022 revision' is factually incorrect. NMPA Order No. 64 is the 2014 GMP; there was no 2022 revision of Order 64. The latest revision is NMPA Order No. 107 (issued Nov 2025, effective Nov 1 2026). This should read 'Order No. 64 (2014)' or be updated to reference the forthcoming No. 107. Gap description accurately identifies the key EU MDR additions: NB audit for IIa+, PSUR/PMCF, vigilance timelines (2/10/30 days), Annex II/III format, English-language QMS. Vigilance timelines (2/10/30 days) are confirmed in MDR Art 87. mandatory_status accurate.

SOURCE URL ISSUE: source.url is a EUR-Lex search URL (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/search.html?...text=60601) — not a canonical document URL. It will return search results, not a stable official document. Should be replaced with the canonical harmonised standards list URL, e.g. the Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/1182 on harmonised standards for MDR (health.ec.europa.eu) or the OJ C publication. FIXED suggestion: https://health.ec.europa.eu/medical-devices-sector/new-regulations/guidance-mdcg-endorsed-documents-and-other-guidance_en or direct to the Commission decision publishing EN 60601-1 as harmonised. STANDARD CONTENT: EN 60601-1:2006+A1:2013+A12:2014 (IEC 60601-1 Ed.3.1) is confirmed as the current harmonised standard for MDR. GB 9706.1-2020 mandatory from 2023-04-01 and its IEC 60601-1 Ed.3 alignment is confirmed. GSPR Annex I presumption-of-conformity mechanism described correctly. Gap analysis (separate EN test report + harmonised-standard declaration required; GB cert alone insufficient; ISO 14971 risk file linkage; different NB/CNAS accreditation bodies) is accurate. mandatory_status: 'Use of harmonised standards is voluntary in principle but practically required' is correct EU law characterisation. CN mandatory status confirmed.

SOURCE URL ISSUE: source.url is again a EUR-Lex search URL (https://eur-lex.europa.eu/search.html?...text=60601-1-2) — not a canonical document. Same fix as medeu-safety-001 applies. FIXED suggestion: point to Commission Implementing Decision harmonised standards list for MDR. STANDARD CONTENT: EN 60601-1-2:2015 (IEC 60601-1-2 Ed.4) EMC standard is correctly described. YY 9706.102-2021 replacing YY 0505-2012, effective 2023-07-01, aligned to IEC 60601-1-2 Ed.4 — confirmed. Ed.4 vs Ed.3 differences (professional vs home healthcare immunity levels, electromagnetic disturbance risk management, intended electromagnetic environment declaration) are correct. OJ citation [UNVERIFIED] self-flag is appropriate. Gap analysis accurate (EU needs EN 60601-1-2 harmonised-standard declaration; ISO 14971 EMC risk integration; different accreditation bodies). mandatory_status: EU voluntary-in-principle/practically-required is correct; CN YY 9706.102-2021 mandatory from 2023-07-01 confirmed.

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). MDR Art 27 UDI assignment (Basic UDI-DI + UDI-DI + UDI-PI) and AIDC+HRI carrier requirements confirmed by official EC UDI helpdesk. EU issuing agencies (GS1, HIBCC, ICCBBA) confirmed. EUDAMED upload before market placement confirmed — UDI/Device module mandatory from 28 May 2026 per Commission Decision (EU) 2025/2371. PHASED DATE ACCURACY: fragment states 'Class III/IIb implantables: 2021; Class IIb/IIa: 2023; Class I: 2025' — official EC dates are 'implantable devices and Class III: 26 May 2021; Class IIa and IIb: 26 May 2023; Class I: 26 May 2025'. Fragment's 'Class IIb/IIa' ordering vs 'Class IIa and IIb' is cosmetically loose but dates are correct. STANDARD CITATION ISSUE in cn_common_equivalent.standards_or_laws: 'YY/T 0287 (ISO 13485 equivalent)' is listed under UDI standards — this is a QMS standard, not a UDI standard; it does not belong in this list for UDI. CN UDI rules should cite NMPA Order No. 19 (2019) and NMPA Announcement 2019 No. 10 which are listed but appear alongside the misplaced YY/T 0287. compliance_verdict is appropriately marked [UNVERIFIED].

SOURCE URL ERROR: source.url is 'https://ec.europa.eu/tools/eudamed/' — this URL is stale/dead. EUDAMED has migrated; the current official page is https://health.ec.europa.eu/medical-devices-sector/eudamed_en and the live EUDAMED portal is at https://eudamed.ec.europa.eu. The old ec.europa.eu/tools/eudamed path no longer serves the authoritative EUDAMED content. FIXED to: https://health.ec.europa.eu/medical-devices-sector/eudamed_en. STANDARD CITATION ERROR: standards_or_laws cites 'Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2017/2185' for EUDAMED actor registration. CIR 2017/2185 is about codes and types of devices for the purpose of specifying NB designation scope — it is NOT the EUDAMED actor/device registration regulation. The correct citation for EUDAMED operational rules is Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/2078 (lays down EUDAMED rules under MDR/IVDR). EUDAMED mandatory status: four modules became mandatory 28 May 2026 per Commission Decision (EU) 2025/2371. Fragment says 'actor registration active since EUDAMED phased rollout 2021–2024' which understates the current full mandatory status. compliance_verdict appropriately marked [UNVERIFIED].

source.url confirmed canonical (MDR 2017/745 EUR-Lex). MDR Art 11 Authorised Representative obligations (non-EU manufacturer, written mandate, joint liability, EUDAMED registration) confirmed by official MDCG 2022-16 and EC AR factsheet. MDR Art 15 PRRC (at least one PRRC, specified qualifications: degree in natural sciences/medicine/pharmacy/engineering + ≥1 year experience OR 4 years relevant experience without degree) confirmed by MDCG 2019-7 (guidance on Art 15). MDR Art 13 importer obligations confirmed by EC factsheet. MDR Art 30 EUDAMED AR registration confirmed. CN domestic agent (境内代理人) described correctly. Gap analysis (AR as jointly-liable legal entity, not mere admin agent; PRRC qualification requirements; importer Article 13 checks) is accurate. mandatory_status correct.
